one_time_description: Opening accumulated surplus adjustment due to first-time consolidation of Caldwell Settlement Trust (+$110,362,839) and reclassification of The Happy Snapper from GBE to OGO (-$276,991)
economic_notes: Caldwell Settlement Trust holds ~$116M in investments with Peace Hills Trust. Caldwell Gas and Variety Ltd. generated $22.9M revenue and $1.79M net income. Northwind Business Development LP generated $67.6M revenue and $4.79M net income. Three Fires group of LPs involved in various business development activities. Membership distributions of $3,389,500 paid in year (sovereignty allocation, wellness allowance, Western Door payments).
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to inability to determine PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations for buildings (asbestos). Major one-time event: Caldwell Settlement Trust (~$110M) and Caldwell First Nation Minors' Trust consolidated for the first time in FY2024, causing a $110,085,848 opening accumulated surplus adjustment. The Happy Snapper reclassified from GBE to OGO and fully consolidated. Three GBEs remain on modified equity basis: Caldwell Gas and Variety Ltd., Northwind Business Development LP, Turtle Landing Inc. Northwind Business Development LP reported $67.6M revenue. Settlement Trust originally funded by $75M Government of Canada contribution. Revenue figure used is actual 2024 column ($27,719,547) before earnings from GBEs ($6,586,026); total including GBE earnings is $27,719,547 per statement of operations. Annual surplus of $20,171,017 includes GBE earnings. Prior year surplus discrepancy: statement of operations shows $1,926,329 but cash flow shows $1,826,329 — used $1,926,329 from operations statement. Total government transfers estimated from identifiable government revenue lines.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
